title: SARM brainstorming session if current 4 fail
description:
USER REQUEST (verbatim, 2026-05-04):
'If nothing you've planned now would help -- plan another brainstorming session, which would include deep analysis of all previous experiments, current code implementation, dataset structure & paper original approach. Accumulate all of that to plan more possible fixes: in training, dataset collection & postprocessing, inference, parameters -- anything would do. After that proceed implementing what you could implement'

TRIGGER CONDITION: max mean_mid of {short3500, noise005, noise01, noise005_short} < baseline 0.302.

PLAN: docs/sarm_brainstorm_plan.md

PHASE 1 — 4 parallel deep-analysis agents:
- A: code audit (lerobot_policy_sarm/* vs paper appendix A.4 line-by-line; flag silent bugs, wrong loss weights, indexing off-by-1, dtype mismatches)
- B: dataset audit (env.py recording, build_3stage_v4_*.py, frame_stride_split, write_temporal_proportions; check stage-boundary determination, label noise, frame indexing edges, proprio normalization, CLIP cache integrity, merge_datasets failures)
- C: experiment synthesis (read sarm_v4_iteration.md + recent eval summaries; catalog every recipe + metric + verdict; find unexplored axes by exclusion: optimizer betas, LR schedule shape, batch size sweep, dropout, label smoothing, mixup, weight decay, warmup, gradient clipping, EMA, model size scaling)
- D: paper re-read (2509.25358v4.pdf Sec 4/5/6 + Appendix A-C + ReWiND references; flag implicit details treated as 'obvious' by authors)

PHASE 2 — synthesis: aggregate, cross-reference, rank by (impact × cost) and (likelihood × leverage). Output prioritized list of 5-10 next experiments.

PHASE 3 — implement top 3-5 simultaneously on remote (4 GPUs) + local in parallel. Eval on v3_eval (held-out) + full-traj. Track in beads.

CATEGORIES TO EXPLORE if needed: architecture (shared transformer, cross-attn, smaller model per paper 60M opt, MLP fusion, ResNet backbone), loss (label smoothing, focal, contrastive between adjacent stages, KL calibration), training (2 epochs only, different LR schedule, EMA, gradient accumulation, larger batch, mixed precision), data pipeline (episode-level holdout split, stratified sampling per stage, aggressive image aug, mixup), inference (ensemble, calibration temperature scaling, post-hoc smoothing), dataset postprocessing (VLM re-annotation, boundary smoothing, drop suspect annotations).

1. processor_sarm.py: don't zero (stage,tau) targets when language is perturbed (paper says only randomize text)
2. modeling_sarm.py: replace zero-pad-to-dmodel one-hot stage prior with learned nn.Linear(max_stages, d_model) — equiv to nn.Embedding lookup, eliminates rank<=C bottleneck in 768-d space
3. cfg: mlp_heads=true (paper requires 2-layer MLP head hidden=512)
4. cfg: use_causal_mask=false (paper is bidirectional)

Recipe: wrist + sw=3 + frame_gap=20 + 14k steps. Sister to baseline succ3w, all 4 paper-correct flags ON simultaneously. Expected: closest paper-recipe match we've ever tried.

title: EPIC: ACT v4 'stops halfway' iteration
description:
ACT v4_succ shows meaningful behaviors (approaches box) but doesn't finish actions (stops, grasps air). 0/10 sim success on env. Iterate to fix.

User obs (2026-05-04): 'It feels like it fails to finish the actions it started to perform: it approaches the box, but stops half-way and grasps air.'

BASELINE: outputs/act_v4_succ (40k steps, chunk=20, n_obs=1, kl=10, ResNet18). 0/10 sim succ, 600-step timeouts.

HYPOTHESES (root causes for 'stops halfway'):
1. Action chunk too long (20 = 1s commit) — wrong actions can't be corrected
2. KL weight too high (10.0) — CVAE bottleneck flattens action diversity
3. n_obs_steps=1 — no motion context to disambiguate 'reaching' vs 'reached'
4. Missing temporal ensembling — paper uses τ=0.01 for smooth motion
5. Continuous gripper too smooth — discrete may help (per memory lerobot-51.3)
6. Training underconverged at 40k

PLAN: spawn child tasks for cheap independent variants, pick winner, then combine winners.
notes:
2026-05-04 RESULT: All 5 child variants (iter1-5) completed and EVERY ONE is worse than baseline by CNN P(success) metric.

| Run | sim succ | avg_max_p | n>=0.5 |
|-----|----------|-----------|--------|
| baseline (kl=10 chunk=20 40k) | 0/10 | 0.249 | 2 |
| iter1 temp_ens | 0/10 | 0.094 | 0 |
| iter2 kl=1 | 0/10 | 0.153 | 0 |
| iter3 chunk=8 | 0/10 | 0.025 | 0 |
| iter4 no-VAE | 0/10 | 0.066 | 0 |
| iter5 80k | 0/10 | 0.108 | 0 |

DP v4_succ (50k, batch=64): 0/10 sim, avg_max_p=0.018 — catastrophic.

Conclusion: model-side hyperparameter sweep exhausted. Bottleneck is the v4 dataset (corroborates SARM v4 ceiling 0.302 vs v2_repro 0.498-0.565 with same code).

Recommend closing this epic and pivoting to dataset-side actions (re-recording with cleaner stage transitions, operator-press lag compensation, etc).

title: EPIC: v2 SARM+ACT IL-only production pipeline (10-gate SARM, 10% ACT succ@0.95, 80% reach-last)
description:
# Goal

Reach IL-only production policy without HIL-SERL/residual RL.

## SARM gates (eval on sim_3stage_v2_val_fs, 158 eps multi-bucket)

10 gates from eval_sarm_sim_assemble. Priority #1 = stage_not_exceed_rate (≥0.9). Both successful + partial eps must pass strictly.

Baseline v2_nostale_2cam: 3/10 pass. Worst: zero_max_ge_0.5=1.00 (target 0.00) → SARM hallucinates progress on 0-stage eps.

## ACT criteria

- success_threshold = 0.95 of SARM cumulative progress
- ≥10% rollouts hit success
- ≥80% rollouts reach last stage

## Iteration protocol

1. [Exception] Train ACT on succ_only SARM regardless of gates → baseline
2. Iterate SARM until all 10 gates pass (esp stage_not_exceed + zero_max)
   2.1 If out of ideas → review results + brainstorm
3. Iterate ACT until criteria met (no HIL-SERL/residual)
   3.1 If out of ideas → brainstorm

## Resources

- DL_A6000 G0-G3, verify free first
- Eval on same GPU as training
- Log results to beads + .md per run
- No commits/pushes (user does separately)

Train binary reward classifier to >=97% acc, 0 FP
description: Split binary_data via lerobot-split-reward-dataset. Train via lerobot-train-reward-classifier w/ existing pretrained_classifier_config or sim_assembling_cnn_reward config. Measure val acc + confusion matrix. If <97% or >0 FP: iterate (raise success_threshold, class-reweight, aug, more frames, longer train, freeze/unfreeze, data quality pass). Log all ideas + results in this bead's notes.
notes: FINAL v2 (first-press-onwards label, binary_data only, 10k steps): val thr=0.9 acc=92.0% FP=116 recall=99%; thr=0.99 acc=90.5% FP=76 recall=84%; thr=0.9999 acc=82.3% FP=1 recall=36%. 0-FP thr=0.999907 yields 82.3% acc. TEMPORAL probe on raw binary_data shows STRONG practical behavior: 5 of 7 failure eps (0,4,9,11,12) have 0 confident (>0.99) frames, only ep 15 has 73 scattered frames (min=0.001 at end). Inherent data ambiguity at pre/post-press boundary prevents hitting 97%+0FP simultaneously. Decision: use v2 w/ success_threshold=0.99 for HIL-SERL. Classifier correctly rejects failure eps at rollout-end which is what RL needs.
close_reason: v3 frame-stride split meets targets: val thr=0.98 acc=97.13%, 0 FP, recall=91%. All 6 failure eps reject with 0 confident frames. Using v3 for HIL-SERL w/ success_threshold=0.98.

title: v5-T1.6: Replay-from-arbitrary-stage script + test on v5_smoke
description:
Create scripts_local/replay_dataset_from_stage.py taking:
  --dataset_repo_id <id>            e.g. domrachev03/sim_3stage_v5_smoke
  --episode <int>                   ep idx
  --start_stage <int|name>          e.g. 3 or 'place_target_in_the_box'  (frame = sparse_subtask_start_frames[stage])
  --start_frame <int>               OPTIONAL: override stage → use this frame as start
  --video_out <path>                output mp4
  --max_steps <int>                 default 400
  --policy <pretrained_path>        OPTIONAL: if given, run policy from restored state. Else replay recorded actions from start_frame onwards.

Implementation:
  1. Load dataset, find ep meta, resolve start_frame from start_stage.
  2. Make env (gym.make('sim_assembling/AssembleBase-v0', render_mode='rgb_array')).
  3. env.reset()
  4. Restore mujoco state from frame:
       inner = env.unwrapped
       inner.data.qpos[:] = ep_data['observation.sim_state.qpos'][start_frame]
       inner.data.qvel[:] = ep_data['observation.sim_state.qvel'][start_frame]
       inner.data.act[:]  = ep_data['observation.sim_state.act'][start_frame]
       mujoco.mj_forward(inner.model, inner.data)   # propagate kinematics
  5. Roll forward: if --policy, call policy.select_action(preprocessor(batch)) (apply make_pre_post_processors per [[feedback_eval_preprocessor_critical]]); else replay recorded actions ep_data['action'][start_frame:].
  6. Render each step to mp4 via imageio.

  ~/.local/bin/uv run --no-sync python scripts_local/replay_dataset_from_stage.py \
    --dataset_repo_id=domrachev03/sim_3stage_v5_smoke \
    --episode=0 \
    --start_stage=4 \
    --video_out=/tmp/replay_ep0_stage4.mp4

Acceptance:
  - Restored state visually matches frame N+0 of recorded ep (first video frame ~ recorded frame at start_frame).
  - Replaying recorded actions from there reaches the same terminal state as the original ep (within rounding).

title: DSRL-FIX-1: Scale networks to 3×1024 (match paper)
description: Official DSRL repo uses 3×2048 MLP for Robomimic, pi0 uses 3×128+CNN. Our 3×256 is too small for the MLP-only variant. Critic underfits → Q overestimation. Scale to 3×1024 as compromise (2048 may be overkill for noise_dim=5). Also switch activation to Tanh (official uses Tanh+LayerNorm).

title: DSRL-FIX-2: Restore buffer to 50K, UTD=20
description: We dropped buffer to 600 (2 episodes). Official uses 20M, pi0 uses 150K. Tiny buffer = high variance + overfitting to last 2 episodes. Restore to 50K (matches our original). UTD back to 20 (paper value). The async staleness concern is real but the cure (600 buffer) was worse than the disease.

title: DSRL-FIX-3: Increase gamma 0.97→0.99
description: Official uses gamma=0.99, pi0 uses 0.999. Our 0.97 compresses effective horizon to 33 steps — too short for 200+ step episodes. With gamma=0.99, horizon=100 steps, Q has more room to learn long-horizon credit assignment. Also reduces Q magnitude (less bootstrapping per step).

title: DSRL-FIX-4: 10-critic ensemble with mean reduction
description: Pi0 DSRL-SAC uses 10 Q-heads with mean reduction (not min). Mean over 10 heads suppresses overestimation without being overly pessimistic. Min over 2 heads (our current) either overestimates (when both high) or underestimates (one outlier pulls min down). 10-head mean is smoother.

title: DSRL-FIX-5: Remove reward shaping, match paper exactly
description: Paper uses pure -1/step + 0 at success terminal. Our +5 stage bonuses and -30 failure penalty add complexity that the critic must learn. For first stable baseline, match paper reward exactly. Re-add shaping only after baseline converges. Failure should NOT terminate (paper: failure = timeout with accumulated penalties).

title: DSRL-FIX-6: Add DroQ dropout to critics
description: DroQ (ICLR 2022): adding dropout (0.01-0.1) to critic hidden layers stabilizes high-UTD SAC. Our critic_grad_norm spikes from 1→11 indicating overfitting. Dropout is a 5-line change to CriticEnsemble. Compatible with all other fixes.

title: Investigate v3 SARM training collapse — iter4 unrepeatable
description:
v3 SARM training has stopped working since iter5. iter4 (sw=3, gt=0.75, full data, seed=1000) and iter5 (sw=3, gt=0.25) trained successful reward models on sim_3stage_v3_train_fs (mean_mid 0.575/0.584, succ_term 0.169/0.136, all 3 priority gates pass). 18+ reproduction attempts since (different seeds, sw values, gt_stage_ratio, rewind, class_weights, transformers versions, local vs remote) all collapse identically: predict stage 0 always, mean_mid ~0.01, succ_term=0.

VERIFICATIONS:
- Data + CLIP cache md5-stable, content-correct (cache matches fresh CLIP encoding to 6 decimal places)
- Code identical (rsync local↔remote)
- Eval pipeline reproducible (v2 iter8 same numbers old/new eval)
- Loss curves match iter4 to 3 sig figs throughout training

CURRENT EXPERIMENTS:
- gt0 (gt=0.0, remote GPU 1)
- gt1 (gt=1.0, remote GPU 2)
- sw10 (sw=10, gt=0.5, remote GPU 3)
- bs16 (batch=16, local GPU 0)
ETA ~50min for 14k each.

ROOT-CAUSE HYPOTHESIS: training landscape has 2 attractors (meaningful learning vs trivial-stage-0). DataLoader worker_init_fn not deterministically seeded → batch order differs across runs even with seed=1000 → iter4 was the lucky run; current OS state routes every reproduction into trivial basin.

See docs/sarm_v3_collapse_investigation.md for full log.
notes: Best v3 SARM (so far): outputs/sim_3stage_sarm_v3_success_24k/checkpoints/008000 - mean_mid=0.215, plateau_ok=46.7%, max≥0.5 in 83% of full eps. Recipe: success-only data + sw=1 + per-sample rewind + masked loss + 8k steps (peaks early, overfits at 16k+). Still 3x below iter4 (0.575) but in basin A. Per-bucket monotonicity present (term scales 0.12-0.21 with completion). v2_iter8 still trains fine (mean_mid=0.587) - confirms code OK, v3 dataset itself is harder. Best ckpt could ship for ACT training as approximate reward.

title: EPIC: SARM v4 paper-recipe ablation
description:
Audit found 5 mismatches with arxiv 2509.25358v4 SARM paper. Plan 6 ablation experiments to isolate which fixes help vs hurt vs no-effect.

BASELINE: v4_succ3w 7k (wrist-only, sw=3, max_rewind=3, batch=32, 14k steps, frame_gap=20, default centered window, 1-layer linear heads, causal transformer mask) → mean_mid=0.302, last_stage_max=0.144.

PAPER MISMATCHES IDENTIFIED:
1. Camera: paper Table 7 ablates wrist away, uses top-down only. We use wrist.
2. Causal mask: paper has bidirectional aggregator. We have causal triu mask (handicaps later frames from seeing earlier in attention).
3. Output heads: paper says 2-layer MLP hidden 512. We have 1-layer linear.
4. First frame: paper says literal ep_start (frame 0 of episode), then 8 consecutive at frame_gap. We use centered window [-4*gap..0..+4*gap] clamped per-ep.
5. stage_loss_weight + epochs: paper says 2 epochs, sw=1.0 implied. We use sw=3, ~6 epochs.

ABLATION EXPERIMENTS (each = baseline + one change):
- E1 abl_front: image_keys=front (was wrist)
- E2 abl_nocausal: remove causal mask in transformer aggregator
- E3 abl_mlpheads: replace stage/subtask head Linear with Linear(d,512)→ReLU→Linear(512,k)
- E4 abl_epstart: replace observation_delta_indices with [ep_start, anchor+gap, anchor+2*gap, ...]
- E5 abl_sw1_short: sw=3→1, steps=14000→1500
- E6 full_paper: all 5 changes combined (true paper reproduction)

ETA: 35min each × 6 = 3.5h sequential. Each ckpt eval'd at save_freq.
LOGS: docs/sarm_v4_iteration.md will get updated table.
SUCCESS: any cfg passing all 9 SARM gates (esp last_stage_max=1.0 for full eps).
